title: Cross-reference closure graph, per-rule closure manifests, and completeness/self-check harness (incl. ZR 11-25 suffix inheritance)
objective: PROPOSED — NOT CONTRACTED (owner approval required to move backlog -> ready). Build a deterministic directed graph over the ingested corpus covering: section-to-section references; definitions imported from §12-10 and other scoped definition sections; §11-25 suffix inheritance; 'except as otherwise provided' relationships; general-to-special regulation priority; exceptions/modifications; special-purpose district overrides; appendices/mapped areas; amendment/supersession relationships. Every releasable rule family gets a closure manifest: entry sections; all reachable controlling sections; imported definitions; applicability predicates; exceptions; overrides; unresolved references; required property facts; source version/hash for every node. A rule may proceed ONLY if all reachable references in its declared scope are resolved; otherwise it returns a typed unsupported/professional_review_required result. An unresolved reference is NEVER treated as non-applicable; searching one chapter cannot satisfy an absence assertion. Cycles terminate deterministically; renumbered/deleted sections are detected. Full spec: project-control/reports/M3-CORPUS-REPLAN-PROPOSAL.md.
business_reason: Completeness must be proven by reachable-reference closure, not assembled manually from one chapter; §11-25 suffix inheritance and §23-422's express suffix exclusion must be modeled before any bare-R5 applicability or absence claim, and before the downstream M4 yard/coverage slices are re-scoped. This is the structural precondition the owner set for regenerating the coverage/rear/front/side slice boundaries.
inputs: M3-T002 accepted: versioned corpus with per-section raw+normalized hashes, cross-reference hrefs, definitions, last-amended/effective dates, amendment/supersession records — the graph's node/edge source
M3-T001 accepted: LEGAL_CORPUS_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md (domains a manifest maps to) + legal_source_manifest schema (per-node version/hash record)

ZR §11-25 (a district designation's regulations apply to a suffixed designation unless the Resolution expressly provides separate provisions); §23-422 express exclusion 'except R5 Districts with a letter suffix'; §12-10 definitions

services/api/app/rules/** (M4-T001 engine): RuleResult coverage vocabulary, rule_conflict/competing-rules trace shape, unsupported/professional_review_required outcomes the closure result must produce — consume unchanged
M4-T006 closure-manifest concept (per-rule reachable set) — the interim informal version this task formalizes
allowed_paths: services/api/app/corpus/closure/** (NEW: graph builder, suffix-inheritance resolver, closure-manifest generator, reachability/cycle-termination)
services/api/tests/fixtures/corpus/closure/** (bounded frozen fixtures: §23-361/R5B, §23-422 suffix exclusion, cyclic ref, renumbered/deleted section, unresolved definition)
forbidden_paths: Editing the rules evaluator core or any ruleset (consume the engine unchanged; this task produces closure inputs, not rules)
Publishing/Verifying any rule; emitting a final value when any reachable reference is unresolved; treating an unresolved reference as non-applicable
Asserting 'confirmed absent' for any modifier; asserting completeness from a single-chapter scan
Editing canonical contracts (property_profile/rule_evaluation/coverage_status/scenario); apps/web/**; project-control/** except own reports; .claude/** except own agent-memory
Adding any new runtime dependency (stdlib graph over the ingested corpus; no graph framework)
acceptance_scenarios: AS-1 suffix inheritance: §23-361 applied to R5B imports §11-25 inheritance unless the section expressly provides a different suffix rule -> R5B inherits R5 provisions where no separate R5B provision exists.
AS-2 express exclusion wins: §23-422's express 'except R5 Districts with a letter suffix' prevents R5 values from leaking to R5A/R5B/R5D; the closure records the express exclusion, not inheritance.
AS-3 unresolved blocks value: an unresolved definition or exception in a rule's reachable set produces NO final value — a typed unsupported/professional_review_required result instead.
AS-4 cycle termination: a reference cycle terminates deterministically (no infinite loop, no arbitrary cutoff that hides a node).
AS-5 renumber/delete detection: a renumbered or deleted section referenced by the graph is detected and flagged unresolved, not silently followed to a stale node.

AS-6 manifest completeness: each releasable rule family's closure manifest lists entry sections, all reachable controlling sections, imported definitions, applicability predicates, exceptions, overrides, unresolved references, required property facts, and source version/hash per node.
AS-7 determinism: identical corpus version -> byte-identical closure manifest + graph.
NC-1 single-chapter absence: a search restricted to one chapter cannot satisfy an absence assertion; the harness asserts that such an assertion is rejected as unsupported.
NC-2 unresolved != non-applicable: a reference the graph cannot resolve is never coerced to 'not applicable' or a default value.

NC-3 bare-R5 not auto-ambiguous: bare R5 in a section with no separate suffix provisions is modeled via §11-25 inheritance (not flagged inherently ambiguous), while a section that expressly lists distinct R5A/R5B/R5D provisions lets those express provisions control.
risks: PROPOSAL ONLY: no dispatch until owner approves the packet + dependency order.
GATES M4: M4-T007 and every later yard/coverage/rear/front/side rule DEPENDS ON accepted M3-T003; the slice boundaries are regenerated from this closure set, not from a hand-assembled chapter list.
LEGAL BOUNDARY: closure resolves references structurally; where resolution requires legal interpretation, the result is professional_review_required, never a guessed edge.
SUFFIX-INHERITANCE CORRECTNESS: §11-25 inheritance vs §23-422 express exclusion is the canonical test pair; getting it wrong silently mis-scopes R5A/R5B/R5D — G3/QA must exercise both.
requirement_refs: project-control/reports/M3-CORPUS-REPLAN-PROPOSAL.md — full spec (owner directive 2026-07-23, sections 3A §11-25 correction, 5 M3-T003, 6 cross-reference-closure harness)
harness_assignments: CROSS-REFERENCE-CLOSURE HARNESS (owner directive section 6): §23-361/R5B imports §11-25 unless expressly overridden; §23-422 express suffix exclusion prevents R5->R5A/R5B/R5D leakage; unresolved definition/exception produces no final value; cycles terminate deterministically; renumbered/deleted sections detected; single-chapter search cannot satisfy an absence assertion.

LOT/APPLICABILITY + RULE-BEHAVIOR + ARCHITECT-BENCHMARK + CONSUMER-BOUNDARY harnesses (owner directive section 6) are ASSIGNED DOWNSTREAM to the M4 yard/coverage slices that consume this closure — M3-T003 provides the applicability-predicate + suffix-inheritance foundation they assert against; it does not itself classify lots or evaluate rules.
